DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Dylan Hackett, Esq.415-410-9931 The Hackett Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Sophia Collins, Esq. 415-276-2561 Littler for Defendant Pilot.

Argued. The Court announces the revised tentative ruling overruling the Demurrer due to an opposition. The Court admonishes Mr. Hackett. Ms. Collins argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT PILOT.COM, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The demurrer is overruled. The complaint adequately alleges a claim for wrongful termination in violation of a public policy (FEHA). The request to strike the opposition is denied. There was no prejudice as the reply responded to the merits of the opposition. Going forward, Plaintiff shall file her papers in a timely fashion and follow all applicable rules

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-04-2023 Continued To Feb-07-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

JESSICA E. QUARLESS, Esq., of LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., (415) 677-3136, for Defendant.

No other appearances.

RE: DEFENDANT PILOT.COM, INC. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Not argued/contested.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's unopposed demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2023 Continued To Dec-27-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
